# MACRO REPORT — 2026-04-24
**Delivered:** 11:45 UTC | **Generated by:** Claude claude-sonnet-4-6

---

## EXECUTIVE SUMMARY

The dominant macro narrative heading into the weekend is the intersection of a fragile US-Iran ceasefire, war-driven energy inflation, and a delayed Fed easing cycle — a stagflationary cocktail that is suppressing risk appetite and complicating central bank mandates globally. Brent crude's sharp -6.69% decline to $98.04 today likely reflects some ceasefire optimism after Trump extended the truce, but triple-digit oil and Strait of Hormuz disruptions remain the single most important variable for today's session, as energy prices are the primary transmission mechanism feeding into inflation expectations, rate paths, and equity valuations simultaneously. The FOMC meeting on April 28-29 will be the week's binary event, with markets now pricing in no cuts until late 2026 following war-driven inflation re-acceleration. [Source: https://www.reuters.com/world/middle-east/fed-rate-cut-pushed-back-late-2026-war-related-inflation-risks-2026-04-22/]

---

## 1. GLOBAL MACRO OVERVIEW

The macro backdrop is defined by a war-driven stagflationary impulse. US CPI stands at 3.32% YoY as of March [Source: https://fred.stlouisfed.org/series/CPIAUCSL], while PPI ran at 1.78% MoM [Source: https://fred.stlouisfed.org/series/PPIACO], both well above the Fed's comfort zone. The Fed funds effective rate sits at 3.64% [Source: https://fred.stlouisfed.org/series/FEDFUNDS], and a Reuters poll of 103 economists now puts a majority favouring no cut before September 2026, with PCE inflation now expected to print at 3.7% in Q2 — roughly 30 basis points higher than estimates from just a month ago. [Source: https://www.kitco.com/news/off-the-wire/2026-04-22/fed-rate-cut-pushed-back-late-2026-war-related-inflation-risks]

The 10-year Treasury yield sits at 4.30% and the 2-year at 3.79% as of April 22, maintaining a normal curve but with the short end constrained by Fed on-hold signals [Source: https://fred.stlouisfed.org/series/DGS10]. S&P Global's April services PMI recovered to 51.3 from a three-year low of 49.8, but the inflation sub-component is described as the most worrying since 2022, with companies paying up for scarce supplies in an echo of pandemic-era dynamics. [Source: https://www.marketwatch.com/story/u-s-inflation-picture-is-the-worst-in-almost-4-years-f3a3b03a]

Equity earnings remain a positive offset. With roughly one-sixth of S&P 500 companies having reported, approximately 85% beat expectations according to FactSet, providing a near-term floor for equities [Source: https://www.wsj.com/livecoverage/stock-market-today-dow-sp-500-nasdaq-04-22-2026]. Today Procter & Gamble beat on both EPS ($1.63 adjusted) and revenue ($21.24 billion) with sales growing 7%, reiterated full-year guidance, and shares rose over 2% premarket — a reminder that corporates with pricing power are navigating the inflationary environment relatively well. [Source: https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/24/procter-gamble-pg-q3-2026-earnings.html]

Globally, central banks are diverging in response to the energy shock. Indonesia held rates to support the rupiah; the Philippines raised rates preemptively; South Africa markets price at least two hikes; and Turkey held steady [Source: https://www.kitco.com/news/off-the-wire/2026-04-22/stocks-fx-dip-us-iran-ceasefire-uncertainty-focus-cenbank-decisions]. The ECB meets next Thursday, April 30, with markets pricing over two rate hikes by year-end 2026, though the pullback in oil prices from near-$103 levels has given the ECB some breathing room to hold at the upcoming meeting. [Source: https://www.reuters.com/business/finance/fragile-hold-five-questions-ecb-2026-04-24/]

---

## 2. CONFLICT / GEOPOLITICAL STATUS

- **US-Iran ceasefire extended by President Trump**, averting an imminent escalation that had been feared as a two-week ceasefire deadline approached; however, diplomacy remains extremely fragile with Trump citing difficulty dealing with a "seriously fractured" Iranian government. [Source: https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/stocks/live/stock-market-today-wednesday-april-22-dow-sp-500-nasdaq-trump-us-iran-ceasefire-230429476.html]
- **Strait of Hormuz disruptions** remain a critical supply bottleneck; traffic through the strait was reported near a standstill, and analysts warned that even if the waterway reopens soon, Gulf oil production will take time to ramp back. [Source: https://www.wsj.com/livecoverage/stock-market-today-dow-sp-500-nasdaq-04-22-2026]
- **Two ships were targeted in the Strait of Hormuz** during the week, keeping oil traders on edge despite the ceasefire extension and pushing Brent toward $103 before today's pullback. [Source: https://www.wsj.com/livecoverage/stock-market-today-dow-sp-500-nasdaq-04-22-2026]
- **VP Vance's trip to Pakistan for Iran peace talks was cancelled**, while a senior Iranian official stated Iran will not attend talks unless the US abandons "threats" — signalling a hardened diplomatic stance. [Source: https://www.cnbc.com/video/2026/04/22/asia-rally-faces-tests-as-geopolitics-ai-and-trade-risks-build.html]
- **US will extend ceasefire while maintaining its blockade**, a posture that keeps oil supply pressure elevated even as immediate escalation risk fades marginally. [Source: https://www.icis.com/explore/resources/news/2026/04/21/11199840/us-will-extend-ceasefire-while-maintaining-blockade]
- **UK inflation jumped to 3.3% in March**, directly attributed to Iran-war fuel price surge — marking the first hard data evidence of the conflict feeding into consumer prices in a G7 economy. [Source: https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/22/uk-inflation-jumps-to-3point3percent-in-march-as-fuel-prices-surge.html]

## 8. WEEK AHEAD / SCENARIO ANALYSIS

| SCENARIO | PROBABILITY | TRIGGER | BRENT | S&P 500 | BTC |
|----------|-------------|---------|-------|---------|-----|
| Bull | 20% | Iran talks produce formal ceasefire framework; FOMC signals rate cut path H2 2026; earnings continue at 85% beat rate | $88 | 7,450 | $88,000 |
| Base | 55% | Ceasefire holds but Hormuz partially disrupted; FOMC on hold, reiterates data-dependence; oil stabilises $93-100; earnings mixed | $96 | 7,100 | $78,000 |
| Bear | 25% | Ceasefire collapses, Hormuz fully blocked; Brent surges above $110; FOMC turns explicitly hawkish; growth scare accelerates | $112 | 6,650 | $68,000 |

### Key Upcoming Catalysts — Week of April 24–30, 2026

1. **FOMC Rate Decision — April 28-29:** The most important binary event of the week. With 56 of 103 economists forecasting no cut through September 2026 and PCE inflation projected at 3.7% in Q2, any hawkish pivot or shift in the dot plot will be a significant risk-off trigger. [Source: https://www.reuters.com/world/middle-east/fed-rate-cut-pushed-back-late-2026-war-related-inflation-risks-2026-04-22/]

2. **ECB Rate Decision — April 30:** Markets are pricing over two ECB hikes by year-end; the ECB's tone on energy-driven inflation and its forward guidance trajectory will determine EUR direction and European equity sentiment. [Source: https://www.reuters.com/business/finance/fragile-hold-five-questions-ecb-2026-04-24/]

3. **US PCE Inflation Data (March) — Expected late April:** The Fed's preferred inflation gauge; consensus now at 3.7% for Q2 PCE. A hotter-than-expected print would cement the on-hold narrative and weigh heavily on rate-sensitive assets. [Source: https://www.kitco.com/news/off-the-wire/2026-04-22/fed-rate-cut-pushed-back-late-2026-war-related-inflation-risks]

4. **US-Iran Diplomatic Developments — Ongoing:** The ceasefire extension is fragile; VP Vance's peace mission was cancelled and Iran has hardened its stance. Any breakdown in talks could send Brent back above $103 and trigger a sharp risk-off move across all assets. [Source: https://www.cnbc.com/video/2026/04/22/asia-rally-faces-tests-as-geopolitics-ai-and-trade-risks-build.html]

5. **Megacap Tech Earnings — Week of April 28:** With ~85% of S&P 500 reporters beating expectations and investors cautious about being underweight ahead of megacap results, these reports will be the key near-term equity catalyst and a significant determinant of Nasdaq 100 direction. [Source: https://www.wsj.com/livecoverage/stock-market-today-dow-sp-500-nasdaq-04-22-2026]

---

- [Fed funds effective rate at 3.64%]: confirmed via https://fred.stlouisfed.org/series/FEDFUNDS

- [S&P Global April services PMI recovered to 51.3 from 49.8; inflation picture worst since 2022]: confirmed via https://www.marketwatch.com/story/u-s-inflation-picture-is-the-worst-in-almost-4-years-f3a3b03a

---

### Disputed

- [Report sources Nikkei, KOSPI, and ASX 200 data to Yahoo Finance/stock market news links]: The report's inline source citation for these Asian indices points to Yahoo Finance ceasefire news articles (e.g., the April 22 stock market live blog), which contain no Asian index data — the actual source is the prices bundle (Yahoo Finance ^N225, ^KS11, ^AXJO as of 2026-04-24). The citations are misleading but the price levels themselves are correct per the bundle.

- [Report states Brent was "near $103" before today's pullback, attributing this to the WSJ April 22 live blog]: The WSJ April 23 live blog actually describes Brent "close to $103 a barrel" on April 23, and the April 22 futures article mentions Brent "just below $94." The report's characterisation that Brent was near $103 and then fell sharply is directionally supported, but the peak of ~$103 appears to have been on April 23 (not earlier in the week as implied). This is a minor timeline imprecision.

- [Report states Brent's -6.69% decline is sourced to the Yahoo Finance ceasefire extension article]: The Yahoo Finance article cited does not contain Brent price data or the -6.69% move. The actual source for Brent's level and % change is the prices bundle (Yahoo Finance BZ=F). The citation is an attribution error.

- [Report cites the Yahoo Finance ceasefire story as source for the S&P 500 closing -0.41% on April 23]: The Yahoo Finance article cited covers April 22 activity (when markets rose ~0.9%), not April 23's -0.41% close. The -0.41% figure is correct per the bundle (as_of: 2026-04-23) but the article cited describes a different session.

- [Report attributes SOL data to Yahoo Finance ceasefire article]: SOL price (+0.07%, $86.20) comes from the prices bundle, not from any Yahoo Finance news article. Citation is incorrect.

- [ETH and XRP sourced to the AlphaPepe/Business Insider article]: That article is promotional content for a speculative presale token and does not contain ETH or XRP price data. The prices come from the bundle (Yahoo Finance ETH-USD, XRP-USD). Citations are incorrect and inappropriate given the source quality.

- [Report implies the ECB meeting is "next Thursday April 30" from the report date of April 24]: This is consistent with the Reuters source and calendar, confirmed correct.

- [Report describes the GE Vernova EPS of $17.44 without flagging the discrepancy]: The CNBC source explicitly notes "it wasn't clear if that [EPS of $17.44] was comparable to estimates of $1.95" — suggesting a possible reporting methodology difference. The report cites this data without flagging the anomaly, which could mislead a trader.
